docs.text:
PYESPN Github

pypi

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
The PYESPN python package hits the hidden espn api and pulls sports data

check out the sports_available and go look at the api endpoints

docs.title: Finding Ids on ESPN.com
docs.text: You can use the espn frontend to find ids to use

docs.title: Testing API Calls
docs.text: You can use postman to test api calls

docs.title: How to Discover ids via ESPN website
docs.text:
This section shows how to get ids from the front end of the espn website

To see sports available go here

docs.title: Player Ids
docs.text: Find or google a player on espn. find the id within the url

docs.title: Team Ids
docs.text: Find or google a team on espn. find the id within the url

docs.title: Event/Game Ids
docs.text: Find or google an event on espn. find the id within the url

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: pyespn requires python 3.11 or higher

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install pyespn

docs.title: Verifying Endpoint in Postman
docs.text:
You can use postman or a tool of the like to test the api. to do this you will need to be able to read into the pyespn.core code to get the makeup of the api calls.

For this example we'll look at a player

docs.title: Finding API URL
docs.text:
if you open up the pyespn.core.players python file you can find the api call

inside the file you can find the function that calls the player info api. you will see here the api call, with the parameterized variables.

you will need to update these parts to get postman to work. within the pyespn.data.leagues python file you can find the LEAGUE_API_MAPPING which has the keys needed to complete the api call

docs.title: Testing in Postman
docs.text: if you go ahead and fill in the variables into the url and run in postman you will see a response with the details for the given player

docs.title: 🏀 Basketball 🏀
docs.text: League Abbreviation League Name mcbb mens-college-basketball nba NBA wnba WNBA

docs.title: 🏈 Football 🏈
docs.text: League Abbreviation League Name cfb college-football nfl NFL

docs.title: ⚾ Baseball / Softball ⚾
docs.text: League Abbreviation League Name mlb MLB cbb college-baseball csb college-softball

docs.title: 🏎️ Racing 🏎
docs.text: League Abbreviation League Name f1 F1 nascar NASCAR Premier indy IRL

docs.title: Create Class
docs.text:
create an init version of the class and feed it the league you want

Param Type Description league string Options: - nfl - nba - wnba - mcbb (mens college cbb) - cfb (college football) - cbb (college baseball) - csb (college softball) - f1 (formula 1) - nascar

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')

docs.title: Team Class
docs.text: The Team class represents a sports team within the ESPN API framework. It maintains a reference to a PYESPN instance to access league-specific details.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description espn_instance PYESPN Reference to the parent PYESPN instance. team_id number Unique identifier for the team. name string Full name of the team. abbreviation string Team abbreviation (e.g., "LAL" for Los Angeles Lakers). location string Geographic location of the team (e.g., "Los Angeles").

docs.title: get_league()
docs.text:
Retrieves the league abbreviation from the associated PYESPN instance.

Returns: - string — The league abbreviation (e.g., "nfl", "nba", "cfb").

docs.title: __repr__()
docs.text:
Returns a string representation of the Team instance.

Returns: - string — A formatted string with the team's location, name, abbreviation, and league.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
from pyespn import PYESPN

nba_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')
lakers = next((team for team in nba_espn.teams if team["team_id"] == 13), None)

print(lakers)  # Output: <Team Los Angeles Lakers (LAL) - nba>
print(lakers.get_league())  # Output: nba

docs.title: Data Files
docs.text:
This is a list of ids/teams in json format that relate to the api and are used in the code.

examples

# this example prints the mapping itself
from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
nba_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')

print(nfl_espn.TEAM_ID_MAPPING) #nfl team map
print(nba_espn.TEAM_ID_MAPPING) #nba team map

this example uses the teams_class

# this example uses the teams class
from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
nba_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')

for team in nfl_espn.teams:
    print(team.name) #nfl team 

for team in nba_espn.teams:
    print(team.name) #nba team

docs.title: Awards Endpoints
docs.text: endpoints available for awards, not available for all sports

docs.title: get_awards(season)
docs.text:
gets awards data for a given season

Param Type Description season number season for rankings

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nba_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')
season = 2024

awards = nba_espn.get_awards(season=season)

for award in awards:
    print(award)

docs.title: Betting Endpoints
docs.text: This section contains all endpoints available for gambling data. this is not available for every league/sport

docs.title: Betting Providers
docs.text:
there are many betting providers across the espn api and they dont appear to be consistent in my profiling. the ones that i have found can be accessed via

there is a mapping for default betting providers for a given sport, it is not guaranteed to return data for every year though

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')

print(nfl_espn.BETTING_PROVIDERS)

docs.title: Against the Spread Endpoints
docs.text: API endpoints available for teams against the spread data, this appears only available for nfl

docs.title: get_team_year_ats_away(team_id, season)
docs.text:
gets a teams record against the spread while away for a season

Param Type Description team_id number id for team season number season for rankings

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
season = 2024
team_id = 30 # JAX 

jax_ats = nfl_espn.get_team_year_ats_away(team_id=team_id,
                                          season=season)

print(jax_ats)

                                                   season=season)

docs.title: Futures Endpoints
docs.text: api calls for futures, not available for every sport

docs.title: get_year_league_champions_futures(season, provider)
docs.text:
gets the lines for the league champion

Param Type Description season number Season for rankings. provider string Betting provider Options can be found in PYESPN.BETTING_PROVIDERS Defaults based on league if not provided.

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nba_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')
season = 2024

nba_futures = nba_espn.get_league_year_champion_futures(season=season)

for future in nba_futures:
    print(future)

docs.title: get_league_year_division_champs_futures(season, division, provider)
docs.text:
gets the lines for the specified division/conf from a provider

Param Type Description season number Season for rankings division string division to get futures for Options can be found in PYESPN.LEAGUE_DIVISION_BETTING_KEYS provider string Betting provider Options can be found in PYESPN.BETTING_PROVIDERS Defaults based on league if not provided.

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nba')
season = 2024
division = 'afc'

nfl_futures = nfl_espn.get_league_year_division_champs_futures(season=season,
                                                               division=division)

for future in nfl_futures:
    print(future)

docs.title: Draft Endpoints
docs.text: draft endpoints available

docs.title: get_draft_pick_data(pick_round, pick, season)
docs.text:
Gets team info from espn api, this is available for pro leagues

Param Type Description pick_round number round of pick pick number pick number in round (not overall) season number season of draft

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
season = 2020
pick = 1
pick_round = 1

draft_pick_info = nfl_espn.get_draft_pick_data(pick_round=pick_round,
                                              pick=pick,
                                              season=season)

print(draft_pick_info)

docs.title: Game/Event Endpoints
docs.text: game/event api endpoints available

docs.title: get_game_info(event_id)
docs.text:
gets event/game details from api. the event ids can be found on the espn site web urls

Param Type Description event_id number id for event 
from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
event_id = 400999172 # Myles Jack wasn't down

super_bowl = nfl_espn.get_game_info(event_id=event_id)

print(super_bowl)

docs.title: Player Endpoints
docs.text: A List of all endpoints available that return player specific data

docs.title: get_player_info(player_id)
docs.text:
gets player level details from api

Param Type Description player_id number id for player

docs.title: Example Use
docs.text:
from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
player_id = 278 # Jimmy Smith, Goat

jimmy_smith = nfl_espn.get_player_info(player_id=player_id)

print(jimmy_smith)

docs.title: Recruiting Endpoints
docs.text: Recruiting endpoints available

docs.title: get_recruiting_rankings(season)
docs.text:
this pulls recruiting data and is currently only works for mcbb and cfb

Param Type Description season number season for rankings

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

cfb_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='cfb')
season = 2020

recruiting_rankings = cfb_espn.get_recruiting_rankings(season=season)

for recruit in recruiting_rankings:
  print(recruit)

docs.title: Standings Endpoints
docs.text: endpoints to get standings

docs.title: get_standings(season, standings_type)
docs.text:
get standings for a given season, this appears only available to racing leagues

Param Type Description season number Season for rankings standings_type string Type of standings Defaults to driver/overall if not provided Options: F1: driver, constructor NASCAR: overall

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

f1_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='f1')
season = 2024
standings_type = 'driver'

f1_standings = f1_espn.get_standings(season=season,
                                     standings_type=standings_type)

for driver in f1_standings:
    print(driver)

docs.title: Stats Endpoints
docs.text: Available Statistics endpoints

docs.title: get_players_historical_stats(player_id)
docs.text:
gets the historical stats for a given player id

Param Type Description player_id number id for player

example

from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
player_id = 278 # Jimmy Smith, Goat

jimmy_smith_stats = nfl_espn.get_players_historical_stats(player_id=player_id)

for stat in jimmy_smith_stats:
    print(stat)

docs.title: Team Data Endpoints
docs.text: available team endpoints

docs.title: get_team_info(team_id)
docs.text:
Gets team info from espn api

Param Type Description team_id number id for team

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
from pyespn import PYESPN

nfl_espn = PYESPN(sport_league='nfl')
team_id = 30 #jax

jags_team_info = nfl_espn.get_team_info(team_id=team_id)

print(jags_team_info)
